source_metadata.title_raw: Caren Ray Russom - A-18-266 - February 1, 2019 - Arroyo Grande

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Caren Ray Russom 
Mayor, City of Arroyo Grande 
756 Myrtle Street 
Arroyo Grande, CA 93420 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-266 
Dear Ms. Ray Russom: 
February 1, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act ("Act") and Government Code Section 1090, et seq. 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and Section 1090, and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common 
law conflict of interest. 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 197 5) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Luis Obispo 
County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive 
a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you 
that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.l(c)(5).) 
QUESTIONS 
1. Under the Act, do you have a conflict of interest in the approval of the development and 
recordation of a "Regulatory Agreement" with the Housing Authority of the City of San Luis 
Obispo ("HASLO")? 
1 Government Code Sections I 090 through I 097 .5. All statutory references are to the Government Code, 
unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 
through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of 
the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-266 
Page No. 2 
2. Under Section 1090, if you recuse yourself, may the City Council approve the 
development and recordation of a "Regulatory Agreement" with HASLO? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. & 2. Yes. Both the Act and Section 1090 prohibit you from making or participating in the 
making of the potential Regulatory Agreement with HASLO; however, the City may enter into the 
agreement pursuant to the remote interest exception set forth in Section 1091 (b )(8), as outlined 
below. Additionally, we note that you must leave the room during the consideration of any such 
agreement pursuant to the Act's recusal requirements. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
You are the Mayor of the City of Arroyo Grande, having been elected Mayor in the 
November 6, 2018 election, and sworn in as Mayor on December 11, 2018. Prior to becoming 
Mayor, you were a member of the City Council, having served in that capacity since 2016. 
The City has a Funding Agreement with HASLO related to affordable housing at 224 S. 
Halcyon Road, Arroyo Grande which was entered into in 2014, prior to your election to the City 
Council. The property has been purchased and the Planning Commission has approved the 
construction of twenty affordable apartments. 
The Funding Agreement includes two conditions that still need to be satisfied. One requires 
obtaining written consent from the City related to the housing not being "Transitional Housing." 
The other requires development and recordation of a "Regulatory Agreement" to restrict rent and 
occupancy for 55 years to low, very low or extremely low-income households. The City will have 
the option of being a party to the Regulatory Agreement, or being a third-party beneficiary, with the 
right to enforce its terms. This Regulatory Agreement will need to come before the City Council in 
the foreseeable future. 
HASLO has sent an email to the City Manager regarding the need to satisfy those conditions 
and mentioned that RRM Design Group are the architects for the project, that construction drawings 
have been submitted and the project is in plan check. Your husband is an architect, and an employee 
of RRM Design Group, which is currently transitioning to an employee-owned company. Although 
RRM Design Group is working with HASLO on architecture and construction documents for this 
project, you state that your husband is not involved in this project. His job title is Senior Project 
Manager and Manager of Architecture at RRM Design Group. 
In a follow-up email, the City Attorney noted that RRM Design Group has a long history of 
working with HA ST ,0 , and has been providing planning, civil engineering, and surveying services 
to HASLO for over a dec?de:__~ 
Design Group has !,een provisfing HASLO with full service 
---~ _ 
·chitectural se.rvices since 2D1LRRM Design Group continues to actively work on HALSO 
projects at various service levels and across multiple jurisdictions. 


ANALYSIS 
The Act 
File No. A-18-266 
Page No. 3 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental 
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one 
or more of the public official's interests. 
Section 87103 defines financial interests to include: 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
$2,000 or more." (Sections 87103(a).) 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, 
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• 
Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution 
made in the regular course of business on terms available to the public without 
regard to official status, aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised 
to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the 
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 
Your personal finances, including those of your immediate family. (Section 
87103.) 
Assuming you meet the financial standards set forth above, you have a community property 
interest in your spouse's income from RRM Design Group. Therefore, you have an economic 
interest in RRM Design Group as a source of income. (Section 87103(c).). Next, we determine if it 
is reasonably foreseeable that a decision will have a material financial effect on your financial 
interest. 
Reasonably Foreseeable and Materiality 
Regulation 18701(a) provides that a decision's effect on an official's interest is presumed to 
be reasonably foreseeable if the interest is a named party in, or the subject of the decision; and an 
interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, 
denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the interest. 
If the official is not explicitly involved in the decision, Regulation 18701 (b) provides that 
the-decision1s-effect-on-the-intt!rt!sl-is-rnasunably-furt!seeable-ifil-can-be-recognized-as-a-realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. 


File No. A-18-266 
Page No. 4 
The standard to determine materiality, for a business entity and business entity as source of 
income, including salary, 2 is set forth in Regulation 18702.1 (b) (business entity, not explicitly 
involved). Materiality is found if "a prudent person with sufficient information would find it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision's financial effect would contribute to a change ... in the 
value of a privately-held business entity." (Regulation 18702.1 (b ).) The regulation notes "decisions 
that may be applicable" to include, as relevant, decisions that: 
(1) Authorize, prohibit, regulate or otherwise establish conditions for an activity in 
which the business entity is engaged; 
(3) Increase or decrease the need for products or services that the business entity 
provides; 
(Regulation 18702.l(b).) 
Based on the facts presented, even if your interest in not explicitly involved in the decision, 
a prudent person with sufficient information would find it is reasonably foreseeable that the 
decision's financial effect would contribute to a change in the value of a privately-held business 
entity, by authorizing the conditions for an activity in which the business is engaged, and increasing 
the need for services that the business entity provides. The city attorney noted that RRM Design 
Group has been providing planning, civil engineering, and surveying services to HASLO, and also 
has been providing HASLO with full service architectural services. RRM Design Group continues 
to actively work on HALSO projects at various service levels and across multiple jurisdictions. The 
ultimate approval of a project for which RRM Design Group provided services would result in a 
successful project. At minimum, this would appear to bolster the reputation of the business and 
likely lead to further opportunities either with HASLO or other clients perusing similar projects. 
That being the case, it is reasonably foreseeable a decision related to the Regulatory Agreement 
would have a material financial effect on RRM Design Group thereby resulting in a conflict of 
interest. 
Note that when a public official holds an office specified in Section 87200 (including 
mayors) has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public meeting, he or she must: 
(1) immediately prior to the discussion of the item, orally identify each type of economic interest 
involved in the decision as well as details of the economic interest on the record of the meeting; 
(2) recuse himself or herself, and (3) leave the room for the duration of the discussion and/or vote 
on the item. (Section 87105.) 
2 See Regulation 18702.3(a)(4). Where the source of income, including salary, is a business entity, Regulation 
18702.3 refers back to the standard applied in 18702.1 for business entities. 


Section 1090 
File No. A-18-266 
Page No. 5 
Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has 
a financial interest." (People v. Honig (l 996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333.) A contract that violates 
Section 1090 is void. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.) Section 1090 is concerned with 
financial interests, other than remote or noninterests, that prevent public officials from exercising 
absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests of their agencies. (Stigall v. 
Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) 
An officer is conclusively presumed to be involved in the making of his or her agency's 
contracts when the officer is a member of a board or commission that has the power to execute the 
contract at issue. (Thomson, supra, at p. 649.) As a general rule, when Section 1090 is applicable to 
one member of a governing body of a public entity, the entire governing body is precluded from 
entering into the contract. (Stigall, supra, at p. 569.) 
Your facts indicate that you have a financial interest in the Regulatory Agreement in light of 
your husband's employment with RRM Design Group as well as RRM Design Group's work on the 
project and other work performed on behalf of HAS LO. You therefore inquire whether you have a 
remote interest under Section 1091 (b )(8) such that the City may nonetheless amend or enter into the 
agreement with HASLO. 
Section 1091(b)(8) establishes that a "remote interest" exists where the official is a "supplier 
of goods or services when those goods or services have been supplied to the contracting party by 
the officer for at least five years prior to his or her election or appointment to office." Thus, an 
official would have a financial interest in a contract entered into by a government entity if he or she 
provides services to the party contracting with that government entity, but only a remote interest if 
those services have been provided for at least five years prior to his or her appointment to the 
relevant government entity. Additionally, we note that the five-year period runs from the board 
member's most recent term, as opposed to the initial term. (86 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 187 (2003).) 
You were elected Mayor in the November 6, 2018 election. According to your facts, RRM 
Design Group has been providing planning, civil engineering, and surveying services to HASLO for 
over a decade, and with full service architectural services since 2011. This is a period of well over 
five years prior to the beginning of your most recent term of office, as required for the application 
of the remote interest exception under Section 109l(b)(8). In the present situation, you would have 
a remote interest in any contract between the City and HAS LO if you were an employee of RRM 
Design Group. Likewise, the remote interest exception under 10901 (b )(8) also applies where your 
spouse is the employee of the contracting party. (78 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen 230 (1995) and 81 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 169 (1998).) 
Accordingly, you will have a "remote interest" under Section 109_1 (b )(8) in the agreement 
the Gity~seel~Ho enter inte with~HASL,Q.;:aaWhen=a "remote~interest" is=present, a=board~may~exeeute 
a contract as long as: (1) the officer in question discloses his or her financial interest in the contract 
to the public agency, (2) such interest is noted in the entity's official records, and (3) the officer 
abstains from any participation in the making of the contract. (Section 109l(a); 83 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 246, 248 (2000).) Therefore, the City Council may enter into a Regulatory 
Agreement with HASLO so long as you disclose your financial interest in the agreement, · 


File No. A-18-266 
Page No. 6 
that disclosure is noted in the official records, and you abstain from any participation in making the 
agreement. 
Additionally, we note that you must also leave the room for the duration of the discussion 
and the vote on the Regulatory Agreement with HASLO to comply with the Act's conflict of 
interest recusal requirements. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
()' 
r . 
. 
- achary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

Obispo ("HASLO")? 
1 Government Code Sections I 090 through I 097 .5. 2. Under Section 1090, if you recuse yourself, may the City Council approve the 
development and recordation of a "Regulatory Agreement" with HASLO?

below. Additionally, we note that you must leave the room during the consideration of any such 
agreement pursuant to the Act's recusal requirements.
